meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 7–8.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Rikizo and Yamakawa chat before class about family members.

lines.jp: りきぞさんは 兄弟が いますか。
はい、兄が います。会社で 毎日 働いて います。
お兄さんは どんな 人ですか。
兄は 家族の 中で いちばん 親切です。

items.question: What does Rikizo say about his older brother?
choices: He is the kindest in the family.
He cooks for the family every day.
items.answer: He is the kindest in the family.
items.explanation: りきぞ says 兄は家族の中でいちばん親切です (my older brother is the kindest in the family).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: Two classmates compare their favourite seasons during lunch break.

lines.jp: 春と 秋と、どちらが 好きですか。
秋が 好きです。木の 色が とても きれいですから。
夏は 暑いですが、夏休みが あって いいですね。

items.question: Why does B prefer autumn?
choices: Because the colours of the trees are beautiful.
items.answer: Because the colours of the trees are beautiful.
items.explanation: B says 木の色がとてもきれいですから (because the colours of the trees are very beautiful).

items.title: Conversation 3
items.context: Two close friends chatting casually at school on a cold morning.

lines.jp: 今日 寒いね。もう 冬だ。
うん。冬は 寒いけど、ゆきが きれいだから 好き！
私は 姉と 冬の ゆきを 見たい。
いいね！お姉さんは とても 親切だよ。

items.question: Why does B like winter?
items.explanation: B says 冬は寒いけど、ゆきがきれいだから好き (B likes winter because the snow is beautiful, even though it is cold).

sections.title: Part 2: Grammar Scramble
sections.instructions: Construct the sentence by clicking the words in the correct order.

items.q: My older sister is better at cooking than my older brother.
items.explanation: 姉 + は (topic) + 兄 + より (than) + 料理 + が (subject of predicate) + 上手です. Use より to express 'more than' in a comparison. を marks a direct object, not relevant here. 弟 (younger brother) is a distractor person; 下手です (bad at) reverses the meaning.

items.q: My family likes autumn the most.
items.explanation: 私の + 家族 + は (topic) + 秋 + が (subject) + いちばん (most) + 好きです. Use いちばん for superlative meaning 'the most'. より is used for comparison between two things, not for superlative. 春 (spring) is a distractor season.

items.q: My older brother works at a company every day.
items.explanation: 兄 + は (topic) + 毎日 + 会社 + で (location of action) + 働いて (te-form) + います (progressive). で marks the place of action. を marks a direct object, which doesn't apply here. 働きます is the simple polite form, not the progressive ています pattern. Time words like 毎日 can also go sentence-initially: 毎日 + 兄は + 会社で + 働いています.

items.q: Winter is cold, but the snow is very beautiful.
items.explanation: 冬 + は (topic) + 寒いですが (cold, but — contrast connector) + ゆき + が (subject) + とても + きれいです. が in 寒いですが connects contrasting clauses. を marks a direct object. 暑いですが reverses the season's temperature. きれいじゃない negates the beauty.

items.q: My older sister is kind and the most liked in the family.
items.explanation: 姉 + は (topic) + 親切で (kind — connective て-form of な-adjective 親切) + 家族の中で (among the family) + いちばん (most) + 好きです. 親切で connects two descriptions with the conjunctive な-adjective form. を marks a direct object and does not fit here.

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — 私の 家族と 好きな きせつ

passage.jp: 私の 家族は 5人です。親と 兄と 姉と 弟が います。
passage.en: There are five people in my family: my parents, older brother, older sister, and younger brother.

passage.jp: 兄と 姉は 今、会社で 働いて います。
passage.en: My older brother and sister are now working at a company.

passage.jp: 姉は 家族の 中で いちばん 親切で、毎日 料理を 作って くれます。
passage.en: My older sister is the kindest in the family and makes food for me every day.

passage.jp: 私の 家族は 秋が 好きです。秋は 夏より すずしくて、木の 色が 赤や 茶色に なります。
passage.en: My family likes autumn. Autumn is cooler than summer, and the colour of the trees turns red and brown.

passage.jp: 冬は 寒いですが、家族で ゆきを 見るのが 好きです。
passage.en: Winter is cold, but the family likes watching snow together.

questions.q: 家族は 何人ですか。
questions.explanation: The first sentence says 私の家族は5人です (there are five people in my family).

questions.q: 家族の 中で だれが いちばん 親切ですか。
questions.explanation: Sentence 3 says 姉は家族の中でいちばん親切で (my older sister is the kindest in the family).

questions.q: 冬に 家族で 何を するのが 好きですか。
questions.explanation: Sentence 5 says 家族でゆきを見るのが好きです (the family likes watching snow together).

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: [家族]は 5人です。
items.explanation: 家族 (family) is read as kazoku. 家 (N5) + 族 (N4.7).

items.q: お兄さんは とても [親切]です。
items.explanation: 親切 (kind) is read as shinsetsu. 親 (N4.7) + 切 (N4.1).

items.q: [兄弟]は 何人 いますか。
items.explanation: 兄弟 (siblings) is read as kyoudai. 兄 (N4.7) + 弟 (N4.7). The on-readings combine: けい/きょう + だい → きょうだい.

items.q: [お姉さん]は 料理が 上手です。
items.explanation: お姉さん (older sister, polite form) is read as oneesan. 姉 (N4.7) with honorific prefix お and suffix さん.

items.q: [夏休み]は 長いですね。
items.explanation: 夏休み (summer vacation) is read as natsuyasumi. 夏 (N4.8) + 休み (rest/break).

items.q: [冬休み]も 好きです。
items.explanation: 冬休み (winter vacation) is read as fuyuyasumi. 冬 (N4.8) + 休み (rest/break).

items.q: 夏は [暑い]ですが、きれいな きせつです。
items.explanation: 暑い (hot, weather) is read as atsui. 暑 (N4.8) — note: this kanji means hot weather/air; 熱い (atsui) is for hot objects.

items.q: 冬は [寒い]ですが、ゆきが あります。
items.explanation: 寒い (cold, weather) is read as samui. 寒 (N4.8) — cold air/weather. すずしい (cool) is a distinct adjective not written with 寒.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 弟は まだ 小さいですが、とても _______ です。
items.explanation: 親切 (shinsetsu, kind) fits: the younger brother is small but kind. 親切 is a な-adjective describing personality. 家族 (family), 兄弟 (siblings), and 親 (parent) are nouns, not personality adjectives.

items.q: 冬に 北では _______ が あります。白くて きれいです。
items.explanation: ゆき (snow) fits: in the north in winter there is snow, which is white and beautiful. みどり (green) is associated with spring nature. 花 (flowers) are spring and summer. きせつ (season) is too general.

items.q: 春、夏、秋、冬を 日本語で _______ と 言います。
items.explanation: 春夏秋冬 (shunkashuutou) is the compound word naming Japan's four seasons together. きせつ (season) is the general word for season, not the specific compound for all four seasons listed together.

items.q: 秋は 夏より _______ です。だから、外に 行きたいです。
items.explanation: すずしい (cool and refreshing) fits: autumn is cooler than summer, making it pleasant to go outside. 寒い (cold) describes winter-level cold. 暑い (hot) describes summer. 親切 (kind) is a personality trait, not a weather condition.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or verb form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 姉 ______ 兄より 料理が 上手です。
items.explanation: Use は to mark 姉 as the topic in a comparison. は sets up 姉 as the subject being discussed, and より follows the comparison point (兄). が would mark 姉 as a new-information subject, but は is more natural when contrasting two family members.

items.q: 家族の 中で 夏が いちばん ______ きせつです。
items.explanation: Use 好きな (attributive form of な-adjective 好き) before the noun きせつ. 好きな modifies a noun: 好きな きせつ (the season I like). 好きです is the sentence-final predicate form. より is used for comparison, not for describing a noun.

items.q: 兄は 今 会社で 働いて ______。
items.explanation: Use 働いて います (te-form + います) to express an ongoing action. 兄は今 (the brother is currently) + 働いて (working, te-form) + います (progressive state). います is for people/living things; ありません is for objects. いました is past progressive.

items.q: 春は 夏 ______ すずしいです。
items.explanation: Use より (than) to make a comparison: 春は夏より = 'spring is [more] than summer'. から marks a starting point ('from'). まで marks an ending point ('until'). だけ means 'only' and does not make comparisons.
